> Confidential | RPX Technologies | March 2026CONFIDENTIAL
> RPX Technologies
> Business Proposal
> From concrete sensors to AI-powered workflow execution
> Recommendation in one sentence
> Launch InSite AI powered by Ruh.ai as an AI workflow layer on top of RPX's InSite platform, prove value 
> inside existing concrete monitoring accounts, and then expand from concrete operations into broader 
> construction workflow automation.
> Prepared for RPXTech leadership / business unit owners
> Date March 6, 2026
> Why this proposal matters
> RPX already owns a trusted data stream at a moment when construction firms are actively evaluating AI.
> The next value unlock is not more raw data; it is faster decisions, cleaner documentation, better customer 
> support, and recurring workflow software revenue. Confidential | RPX Technologies | March 20261. Executive summary
> RPX Technologies already captures high-value concrete data through its InSite hardware, cloud software, 
> alerts, reports, and customer support workflows. The proposed move is to turn that data advantage into an 
> AI workflow business: an operating layer that monitors project context, drafts actions, compiles 
> documentation, supports customers, and keeps a full audit trail.
> The recommended launch is not a generic construction chatbot. It is a concrete-specific AI product - 
> branded initially as InSite AI powered by Ruh.ai - focused on the repetitive tasks already surrounding RPX's 
> data: monitoring summaries, threshold-based notifications, QA/QC documentation, deployment guidance, 
> and customer support.
